---
title: "Development"
---

## Local Setup

```bash
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest -q
```

Build and check package artifacts:

```bash
python -m build --sdist --wheel
python -m twine check dist/*
```

## Documentation

The documentation source is a Quarto website in `docs/`.

```bash
quarto preview docs
quarto render docs
```

Rendered files are written to `docs/_site/` and are not committed.

The docs are written as a student-focused practical explainer for PU/DO
combinatorics. Prefer abstract integer examples for the main path. If custom
labels are needed, use compact labels such as `1 -> "1'"` rather than
domain-specific rider or vehicle names.

Keep the package boundary clear:

```text
pudo-sequences: generate PU/DO-valid event orders
application: check capacity, time windows, vehicle load, cost, energy, reward
```

Mermaid diagrams can be used directly in Quarto. Prefer vertical diagrams with
short block labels so they render well in narrow documentation layouts.

````markdown
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A["new PUDO<br/>requests"] --> B["PU/DO-valid<br/>orders"]
    B --> C["capacity<br/>check"]
    C --> D["score and<br/>select"]
```
````

The API page contains one hidden executed Quarto profiling chunk. It is frozen
with `freeze: auto` so benchmark output is reused until the page source changes.
The chunk is intended for maintainer publishing, not for ordinary package users.

## Release Checklist

1. Update code and tests.
2. Run `pytest -q`.
3. Run `quarto render docs`.
4. Update the version in `pyproject.toml` and `pudo_sequences/__init__.py`.
5. Rebuild with `python -m build --sdist --wheel`.
6. Check artifacts with `python -m twine check dist/*`.
7. Publish a GitHub release so the release workflow can publish to PyPI.

PyPI releases are immutable, so every public upload needs a new version.
